top_classes.name: Kragero, Norway via the Smithsonian (sample no. NMNH117329).
Calumet Island, Ottowa River, Canada via the Smithsaonian(sample no. NMNH 78662)
Canada via the Smithsonian (sample no. NMNH 117611)
Sample from St. Lawrence Co., New York via the Smithsonian(sample no. NMNH C2393).
Hunt and Salisbury Collection (from the Clay Mineral SocietyRepository #CCA-1), orign, Flagstaff Hill, El Dorado Co., California.
The Smithsonian (NMNH 83369), donated by Jim Crowley(USGS). Sample originally from Tilly Foster Mine, Brewster, New York.
Sample from Baja California, Mexico via Smithsonian (sampleno. NMNH 112513-2.
W. J. Ehmann #40, from Kyogle, N.S.W., Australia.
Donated by W. J. Ehmann , Sample #7 from Castle Creek,Idaho.
Sample from Champo, Isere, France, via the Smithsonian(sample no. NMNH 46393).

Sample was a fragment of a black crystal, contaminated on one end with a clay coating and surficial quartz crystals, this surficial contamination was scraped off. There appeared to be a small amount of internal contamination by a fibrous mineral. Very few grains showed mild alteration, there was about 1% contamination by a low refractive index mineral with low birefringence. Particle size was 74-250 micrometers.(Amphibole Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.inosilicate.coarse.hornbl2.spectrum.txt

Sample was a cluster of several large, deep green crystals. A small amount of contamination was found in thin veins along cleavage planes. Microscopic examination revealed newr purity with less than 1% impurities of low index veins. An index of refraction of <1.64 and pleochroism (green to greenish brown) indicated that this is probably magnesian hornblende. Particle size was 75-250 micrometers. (Amphibole Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.inosilicate.coarse.hornbl3.spectrum.txt

The sample consisted of a single 21.41 g piece of euhedral crystal, translucent deep brown in color. A very small amount of calcite contamination was removed with HCl. Microscopic examination showed the sample to be virtually all pure, clean tremolite, with a very small amount (<1%) of low efractive index impurity.Particle size was 75 - 250 micrometers. (Chain Silicates), (Amphibole Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.inosilicate.coarse.tremol2.spectrum.txt

Sample consisted of two green subhedral crystals 1-2 cm in length and width. Both crystals appeared pure in hand specimen. Under the petrographic microscope, the powdered sample displayed one small, moderately birefringent grain with parallel extinction. There may be minor exsolution. Very little alteration, but a trace amount (<< 1%) of sericite can be seen along cleavage planes. Sample was 110 crystal face. (Chain Silicates) (Pyroxene Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.inosilicate.solid.diopsi1.spectrum.txt

The sample was made up of green chunks of coarsely crystalline material. Microscopic examination indicated that it was pure. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers.(sheet silicates) (Chlorite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.phyllosilicate.coarse.clinoc2.spectrum.txt

The sample was a green micaceous mass about 3 cm square.(sheet silicates) (Chlorite Group)Sample measured had cleavage face. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.phyllosilicate.solid.clinoc1.spectrum.txt

The sample was one large (2.5 cm x 2 cm x 2 cm) crystal with good crystal faces, weighing 19 g. It was dark gray in color, with <<1% limonitic surface contamination, which was easily removed. Under the microscope the sample appears to be pure and free from alteration.(Epidote Group) Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.sorosilicate.coarse.clinoz1.spectrum.txt

The sample was a white crystalline mass. Petrographically the sample appears to be pure chabazite. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers.(Framework silicates) (Zeolite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.tectosilicate.coarse.chabaz1.spectrum.txt

The sample is a cream-colored earthy mass. Because of the earthy nature of this mineral sample, there is little difference between the spectra of different particle size ranges. Petrographically it is clean and pure clinoptilolite. Particle size was 74-250 Micrometers.(Framework silicates) (Zeolite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.tectosilicate.coarse.clinop1.spectrum.txt

The sample was a 3 cm x 3 cm x 4 cm, white, granular mass weighing 30 g. One surface was stained and this material was discarded after crushing. Microscopic examination indicated pure anhydrite. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.sulfate.none.solid.anhyd1.spectrum.txt
